Session Name: The AI Landscape, Computing & Lab Setup
Sub Topics / Learning Objectives: Understand AI context, career paths, developer environment, and core computing/programming concepts

Session Name: Developer Toolkit: Terminal, Git & Python Basics
Sub Topics / Learning Objectives: Master developer toolkit (CLI + version control) and core Python programming building blocks
Lists, Tuples, Sets, dictionaries - Basic opeartions, Functions
Learn how to automate logic using loops and conditions.
Write reusable code and work with structured data formats.

Session Name: Master class: Numbers, Logic & Structure — The Mathematical Language of Data
Sub Topics / Learning Objectives:
Number Systems & Boolean Logic : How computers represent truth. Binary numbers, logical operators (AND, OR, NOT), truth tables, De Morgan's laws. Why this underlies every conditional and every data type.

Set Theory & Mathematical Structures : What is a set, subset, union, intersection, complement. How lists, dictionaries, and JSON are just applied set-theoretic structures. Venn diagrams. Mapping and functions in the mathematical sense (domain, codomain, range).
Learn high-performance data handling with NumPy and Pandas.
Prepare clean, reliable datasets for analysis and modeling.
Understand how data querying logic translates across tools.
Learn aggregation logic across Pandas and SQL.

Session Name: Master class:From Tables to Relationships - The Mathematics of Data Organisation
Sub Topics / Learning Objectives:
Coordinate Geometry & Visualisation Intuition :  The Cartesian plane. What it means to "plot" data. Why a scatter plot is just mapping two variables to (x, y) coordinates. Slope of a line  rise over run,  as the seed of everything in ML.

Basic Descriptive Statistics : Mean, median, mode, range, variance, standard deviation. What each tells you and what it hides. Why the mean lies when data is skewed. Real examples from datasets.
Master intermediate querying and joins for analytics.
Learn to explore data and communicate insights visually.
Connect code to external services and build demo apps.
Consolidate learning and understand ethical data usage.

Session Name: The ML Workflow, Data Prep & Reliability
Sub Topics / Learning Objectives: Understand the end-to-end ML workflow and prepare reliable datasets.
Learn how to build models that generalize well to unseen data.
Master numeric prediction models and control model complexity.
Learn to measure and diagnose regression model errors.

Session Name: Master Class: The Mathematics Behind Learning: Lines, Curves & Errors
Sub Topics / Learning Objectives:
Linear Equations & The Equation of a Line:  y = mx + c. What the slope and intercept represent in real data. How Linear Regression is literally finding the best line through points. Residuals - the gap between the line and the point - as "error."

Calculus Intuition , Rate of Change & Minimisation : What a derivative means conceptually (how fast is something changing?). Why do we want to minimise errors? The idea of a minimum of a curve — where the slope becomes zero. Gradient descent as "walking downhill"  no formulas required, only the picture.
Learn core classification algorithms and decision logic.
Understand ensemble methods and variance reduction.
Measure and interpret classification performance correctly.
Discover structure in unlabeled data.

Session Name: Master Class Probability & Counting :The Mathematics of Uncertainty
Sub Topics / Learning Objectives:
Probability Fundamentals :  Sample space, events, P(A), complement rule, addition rule P(A∪B), multiplication rule for independent events. Classical probability with coins, dice, cards. Conditional probability P(A|B)  how knowing one thing changes the probability of another.

Bayes' Theorem from First Principles : Derived step by step from conditional probability. One concrete real-world example worked out by hand (disease test or spam). The idea that prior belief + new evidence = updated belief. Stated as a formula, not a model.
Simplify data and handle time-dependent modeling challenges.
Consolidate ML knowledge and prepare for GenAI concepts.
Understand how LLMs work and how to control them with effective prompts.
Learn to programmatically interact with LLMs safely and responsibly.
Understand how to ground LLMs using retrieval and semantic search.
Build an end-to-end grounded GenAI application.

Session Name: Master Class: Vectors & Linear Algebra  The Mathematics of Space and Direction
Sub Topics / Learning Objectives:
Vectors from Scratch : What is a scalar vs a vector. Representing vectors as arrows and as coordinate lists. Vector addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication geometrically and algebraically. Magnitude using the Pythagorean theorem. Unit vectors.

Dot Product & Angle Between Vectors  : Formula: a·b = |a||b|cos(θ). Computing it by hand. What the result tells you  positive means the same direction, zero means perpendicular, negative means opposite. Cosine of an angle derived from the dot product formula step by step.
Learn how autonomous agents reason, act, and retain state.
Learn to structure and control agent workflows.
Enable agents to interact with software and return reliable data.
Design safe and controllable agent systems.
Use AI to accelerate development and explore non-proprietary models.
Build GenAI systems that work across text, audio, and vision.

Session Name: Master Class : Calculus Intuition - Change, Slope & Optimisation
Sub Topics / Learning Objectives:
The Idea of a Function & Rate of Change  What is a function (input → output). Plotting simple functions (y = x², y = x³). The slope of a straight line as rise/run. Average rate of change between two points. What happens as those two points get closer  the intuition of a derivative without formal limits.

Derivatives  What They Mean Geometrically  The derivative as the slope of a curve at a point. Tangent line vs secant line. Basic derivative rules: d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ⁻¹, constant rule, sum rule  applied to polynomials only. Finding where a function is increasing, decreasing, or flat (derivative = 0). What a minimum and maximum look like.

Optimisation Intuition — What does "minimise something" mean mathematically? Finding the minimum of a parabola y = x² by setting dy/dx = 0. The idea of "walking downhill"  why the slope tells you which direction to move. Chain rule mentioned conceptually  not computed.
